title: European Central Bank taps 36 payment providers for yearlong digital euro pilot
blurb: The European Central Bank selected 36 payment service providers, including Deutsche Bank, UniCredit, Revolut, Stripe, and Adyen, for a 12-month digital euro pilot beginning in the second half of 2027. The beta will test person-to-person and person-to-business payments online and offline across the ECB and 19 national central banks, advancing technical preparations for a potential retail CBDC.

title: Sources: Stripe and PE firm Advent International have jointly offered $60.50/share to acquire PayPal, a 28% premium to Tuesday's close, valuing it at $53B+ (Milana Vinn/Reuters)
blurb: Stripe and PE firm Advent International jointly offered $60.50 per share to acquire PayPal, a 28% premium to Tuesday's close that values the payments company at more than $53B, according to Reuters sources. A Stripe-PayPal combination would consolidate two of the largest payment processors under one owner, folding PayPal's branded checkout and Venmo into Stripe's infrastructure while drawing near-certain antitrust scrutiny.

title: Visa, Mastercard and Ripple back x402 as agent payments average 32 cents
blurb: The Linux Foundation launched the x402 Foundation with 40 governing members, including Visa, Mastercard, American Express, Stripe, Adyen, Fiserv, Ripple, and Circle, formalizing the HTTP 402 protocol Coinbase built to let software agents pay servers in USDC without accounts or card rails. The standard settled roughly $24M across 75 million mostly sub-dollar transactions last month, averaging 32 cents, positioning stablecoins as the default settlement layer for machine-to-machine commerce.

title: Japan’s biggest card network taps Circle to bring stablecoins to 40 million merchants
blurb: JCB, Japan's largest card network with 140M users and 40M merchants, signed an MOU with Circle to explore USDC for cross-border payments and in-store merchant transactions. The partnership starts with a proof of concept for JCB's internal fund transfers, aiming to cut remittance costs and ease currency-exchange friction for inbound tourists whose bank cards carry spending limits stablecoins can bypass.

title: ECB picks firms including Deutsche Bank, Revolut for digital euro pilot
blurb: The ECB selected 36 banks and payment firms, including Deutsche Bank, Revolut, Adyen, and Worldline, for a 12-month digital-euro pilot starting in the second half of 2027, testing a beta CBDC across online, offline, in-store, and e-commerce payments. The push toward potential 2029 issuance is driven by the ECB's view that dollar-backed stablecoins like USDT and USDC threaten Europe's monetary autonomy, though enabling legislation is still moving through the EU.

title: CFTC orders Kalshi to honor Michigan trades, escalating clash with state regulators
blurb: The CFTC ordered Kalshi to honor trades made by Michigan residents, overriding a state court order that had directed the prediction-markets platform to cancel sports-related event contracts. The move sharpens a jurisdictional fight in which the CFTC, asserting exclusive authority over registered Designated Contract Markets, has already sued Michigan, Connecticut, Illinois, New York, and six other states treating these venues as illegal gambling.

title: Financing the AI boom: from cash flows to debt [pdf]
blurb: A Bank for International Settlements bulletin documents how AI infrastructure spending is shifting from being funded by tech-company cash flows to debt, as hyperscalers and data-center operators tap bond markets and private credit to cover capex. The BIS flags that this growing leverage concentrates risk in a handful of large borrowers, raising financial-stability questions if AI revenue growth fails to keep pace with the buildout.

title: Tether Leads $7M Series A in Pact Labs for USA₮ Payroll
blurb: Tether led a $7M Series A in Pact Labs to route its US-regulated USA₮ stablecoin into payroll and wage-access flows rather than crypto trading alone. The check is small next to Tether's $200M Whop and $16M Transak bets, but it fits a pattern of using strategic investments over direct product builds to give the token recurring, employer-tied transaction volume.

title: JPMorgan says Hyperliquid's rise threatens Circle's USDC economics
blurb: JPMorgan cut its earnings forecasts for Circle and Coinbase, arguing that Hyperliquid's revamped USDC deal weakens the economics of the stablecoin and poses a larger long-term threat to Circle. Under the new terms, Coinbase treats Hyperliquid's ~$6B in USDC as on-platform and pays out 90% of reserve income, creating what JPMorgan calls a prisoner's dilemma that pits the two partners against each other for distribution.

title: Binance bets on becoming a crypto 'super app' as stablecoins reshape growth
blurb: Binance is refocusing its growth strategy from pure crypto trading toward becoming a payments-driven "super app," with stablecoins positioned as the primary engine of that shift, per spot and derivatives head Shunyet Jan. The pivot tracks a broader convergence, as banks and payment firms adopt stablecoins as settlement infrastructure rather than a trading instrument, putting Binance in direct competition with Coinbase's own super-app ambitions.

title: US-UK transatlantic taskforce unveils digital asset roadmap promoting stablecoin innovation
blurb: The US Treasury and HM Treasury released a joint statement through their Transatlantic Taskforce, calling on the FCA, Bank of England, SEC, and CFTC to develop shared approaches for tokenized assets and stablecoins. The framework pushes for priority legal claims on reserves for stablecoin holders in insolvency and cross-border capital-raising options, arriving as the GENIUS Act marks its first year in force.

title: Dragonfly, FirstMark lead $38 million Series A for stablecoin startup Velocity with support from Coinbase, Ripple and more
blurb: Velocity, a stablecoin treasury and settlement platform, raised $38M in a Series A led by Dragonfly and FirstMark, with Coinbase Ventures, Ripple, Wintermute Ventures, QED, and Capital One Ventures participating. The platform lets enterprises hold, move, and settle funds in stablecoins while connecting to traditional banking rails and compliance systems, cutting settlement times and removing prefunding requirements for cross-border capital movement.

title: Lummis Says CLARITY Act Text Coming 'in Next Few Days'
blurb: Sen. Cynthia Lummis said she will introduce CLARITY Act text within days, pushing the Senate's crypto market-structure bill toward a floor vote before the August 7 recess deadline. Passage still hinges on unsettled Democratic support for the 60-vote threshold, with only two Democrats committed and banking groups objecting that the bill's stablecoin yield provisions fail to stop stablecoins from substituting for bank deposits.

title: Some U.S. Senate Democrats come out against Clarity Act, calling it a 'corrupt' bill
blurb: Three Senate Democrats, Chris Murphy, Chris Van Hollen, and Jeff Merkley, publicly opposed the Digital Asset Market Clarity Act at a Capitol Hill press conference, calling it "corrupt" unless it bars senior officials, including President Trump, from personal crypto dealings. The bill needs 60 Senate votes and thus significant Democratic support to pass, so the ethics standoff threatens to stall crypto market-structure rules before Congress breaks for the midterm campaign.

title: Tether leads $7 million round in Pact Labs to boost USAT stablecoin adoption
blurb: Tether led a $7M Series A in Pact Labs to build out infrastructure for USAT, its GENIUS Act-compliant stablecoin aimed at payroll, earned wage access, and everyday U.S. payments. The investment targets faster wage settlement and 24/7 employer payouts, positioning USAT, whose supply topped $140M in April, against traditional batch-processing rails that delay worker pay.

title: Ethereum Foundation spinout EthSystems targets banks with blockchain privacy technology
blurb: Former Ethereum Foundation researchers from its Institutional Privacy Task Force launched EthSystems, a for-profit startup building confidentiality infrastructure for banks and asset managers on Ethereum. The company plans to commercialize work started inside the foundation, including confidential stablecoin transfers, private bond issuance, and cross-chain settlement, one of several EF spinouts joining EthLabs and Ethereum Institutional amid a broad reorganization.

title: Coinbase Matches Robinhood's 7% Yield With a Different Design
blurb: Coinbase launched a High Yield USDC tier paying about 7.02% APY, roughly double its Core rate, days after Robinhood Earn debuted a competing 7% campaign, with both routing deposits through Morpho vaults curated by Steakhouse Financial. The two rates are engineered differently: Robinhood tops deposits up to a fixed 7% target for a year, while Coinbase's MORPHO-boosted rate floats with funding markets and drifts lower as TVL grows.

title: Flex doubles valuation to $1.2B in 6 months as $70M Series B1 backs global private banking push for middle-market owners
blurb: Flex raised $70M in a Series B1 led by Halo Fund, doubling its valuation to $1.2B seven months after its December Series B, alongside the launch of Flex Global, a stablecoin-powered banking service in over 100 countries. The AI-driven private bank for middle-market business owners says annualized revenue has tripled to nine figures and plans to nearly double headcount to over 200 by year-end.

title: NYC-based Hadrius, which provides AI-native compliance tools for financial services, raised a $22M Series A led by CRV with participation from Y Combinator (Ryan Lawler/Axios)
blurb: Hadrius raised a $22M Series A led by CRV, with participation from Y Combinator, to build AI-native compliance tooling for financial-services firms. The round targets the KYC/AML and regulatory-reporting workload that RIAs, broker-dealers, and fintechs still handle manually, positioning automated compliance as an operating cost banks and startups increasingly want to shed.

title: Mizuho downgrades Circle to underperform, cuts price target to $50 on Open USD threat
blurb: Mizuho downgraded Circle to underperform from neutral and cut its price target to $50 from $85, citing OpenUSD's yield pass-through model, which distributes most reserve income to issuers and distributors rather than retaining it as USDC does. The bank trimmed its 2027 adjusted EBITDA forecast to $699M, about 25% below consensus, and flagged Circle's August revenue-sharing renegotiation with Coinbase as an added margin risk.

title: The AI boom just found two new winners: Goldman Sachs and JPMorgan Chase
blurb: Goldman Sachs and JPMorgan Chase each posted record quarterly revenue, with Goldman up 39% to $20.3B and JPMorgan up 27% to $58B, driven by AI-fueled trading, IPOs, and deal activity. The results show the AI capex super cycle is now flowing into bank balance sheets through data-center financing, debt and equity underwriting, and advisory work, a multi-year cycle Goldman says is still in its early stages.

title: Cybersecurity stocks rally on AI spending change comments from IBM's Krishna
blurb: Cybersecurity stocks rallied Tuesday after IBM CEO Arvind Krishna said fears over advanced AI models, including Anthropic's Mythos, are pushing customers to pause deals and rethink cyber spending. CrowdStrike jumped 12%, Okta and Netskope each climbed about 11%, and Palo Alto Networks, Zscaler, SentinelOne, and SailPoint each gained roughly 7% as investors bet AI-driven threats will lift security budgets.

title: U.S. June CPI fell 0.4%, likely cooling move toward Fed rate hikes
blurb: U.S. headline CPI fell 0.4% in June against forecasts for a 0.1% decline, while core CPI came in flat versus an expected 0.2% rise, all but ending expectations for a late-July Fed rate hike. Two-year Treasury yields dropped seven basis points to 4.19% and July hike odds had spiked to 42% from 8% a month earlier, per CME FedWatch, ahead of Fed Chair Kevin Warsh's testimony to Congress.
